2604.12736.title: Token-Level Policy Optimization: Linking Group-Level Rewards to Token-Level Aggregation via Sequence-Level Likelihood
2604.12736.paper_summary: Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) has significantly advanced the reasoning ability of large language models (LLMs), particularly in their mathemat ical reasoning performance. However, GRPO and related entropy regularization methods still struggle with token-level sparse-rewards, which is an inherent chal lenge in chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning. These approaches often rely on undifferen tiated token-level entropy regularization, which easily leads to entropy collapse or model degradation under sparse token rewards. In this work, we propose TEPO, a novel token-level framework that (1) leverages sequence-level likelihood to link group-level rewards with individual tokens via token-level aggregation, and (2) introduces a token-level KL-Divergence mask constraint that targets tokens with positive advantages and decreasing entropy to mitigate abrupt policy updates. Experiments demonstrate that TEPO not only achieves state-of-the-art performance on mathematical reasoning benchmarks but also markedly enhances training stability, reducing convergence time by 50% compared with GRPO/DAPO.
2604.12736.paper_summary_zh: 群體相對政策優化（GRPO）顯著提升了大型語言模型（LLMs）的推理能力，特別是在數學推理表現上。<lb/>然而，GRPO及相關的熵正則化方法在標記級稀疏獎勵方面仍然面臨挑戰，這是鏈式思考（CoT）推理中的一個固有問題。<lb/>這些方法通常依賴於未區分的標記級熵正則化，這在稀疏標記獎勵下容易導致熵崩潰或模型退化。<lb/>在本研究中，我們提出了TEPO，一種新穎的標記級框架，該框架（1）利用序列級似然性通過標記級聚合將群體級獎勵與個別標記聯繫起來，並且（2）引入了一種標記級KL散度掩碼約束，針對具有正優勢和降低熵的標記，以減輕突發的政策更新。<lb/>實驗表明，TEPO不僅在數學推理基準上達到了最先進的性能，還顯著增強了訓練穩定性，與GRPO/DAPO相比，收斂時間減少了50%。
2604.12736.author: Xingyu Lin et.al.
2604.12736.authors: Xingyu Lin, Yilin Wen, Du Su, Jinchang Hou, En Wang, Wenbin Liu, Chenfu Bao, Zhonghou Lv
